steps.description: Mix CaHPO4, Ca(OH)2 and CaF2 in stoichiometric ratio (Ca/P = 1.67, mole ratio of calcium to phosphorus = 5:3) with 20 wt% TiO2. Total powder mass = 6 g.

Mechanical activation (ball milling) of the powder mixture for 5 h, 10 h, or 15 h at room temperature under air atmosphere. Ball-to-powder weight ratio (BPR) = 20:1, total powder mass = 6 g, rotational speed = 600 rpm. No process control agent (PCA) used. Polyamide-6 vials (125 mL) and zirconia balls (20 mm diameter).

equipment.name: High-energy planetary ball mill
equipment.settings: 600 rpm, BPR 20:1, polyamide-6 vials (125 mL), zirconia balls (20 mm diameter)

steps.description: Milled specimens were filled in a quartz boat and annealed under atmospheric pressure at 700 °C for 2 h. Heating rate from room temperature to 700 °C was 10 °C/min.

synthesis.notes: The reaction is: 6CaHPO4 + 3Ca(OH)2 + CaF2 + TiO2 → Ca10(PO4)6F2 + TiO2 + 6H2O. Three milling durations were studied: 5 h, 10 h, and 15 h. High phase purity FAp-TiO2 composite was obtained after 15 h of milling. After annealing at 700 °C, crystallinity degree reached a maximum of ~88.79% for the 10-h milled sample. No process control agent (PCA) was used. Zirconia balls and polyamide-6 vials were chosen to minimize contamination.
